perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) days after award and/or completion of the IFB, or upon demand by the County , all Confidential Information, including all photographs, sketches, models, and drawings related to the desi gn, layout and operation of said project shall be destroyed and files deleted from all of the Bidder’s web sites and electronic devices (including but not limited to computers, cellular phones, etc.). Bidder shall also verify that all Confidential Informa tion provided to partners, subcontractors, agents and clients has Confidentiality & Non -Disclosure Agreement RFQ 205 – Construct ion of the Ashburn Sheriff Station Page 2 of 3 been destroyed. The successful Bidder shall destroy all Confidential Information within ten (10) days afte r final Project completion.
perturbation.changed_text: The Bidder may, at its discretion, retain Confidential Information indefinitely. There is no requirement to destroy any materials, digital or physical, related to the project, and no need to verify destruction by partners, subcontractors, agents and clients.
perturbation.explanation: This modification eliminates the requirement to destroy confidential information. It directly contradicts the purpose of the agreement stated in section 1 which is to maintain the confidentiality and security of project details. It also contradicts the requirement in section 4 that confidential information is to be used in strict confidence solely for the purpose allotted under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The purpose of this agreement is to allow the Bidder access to Confidential Information as defined herein detailing the design of the Project for the sole purpose of evaluating the feasibility of potential partnerships related to the Project. This agreement is not intended to ensure the safety and security of the Ashburn Sheriff Station. By signing this agreement, the Bidder is stating that it will handle Confidential Information with reasonable care. Further, the Bidder warrants that its employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients (collectively hereinafter the “Bidder”) will be bound only by the minimal requirements of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the destruction of confidential information, whereas the changed text permits the bidder to retain it indefinitely. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text which states the bidder will handle confidential information with reasonable care and warrants that its employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients will be bound only by the minimal requirements of confidentiality. The changed text fundamentally undermines the agreement's purpose, establishing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Bidder agrees to indemnify and h old harmless the County, its elected officials, employees and agents from and against any and all liability, damages, claims, suits, liens, and judgments (including reasonable attorney’s fees), of whatever nature, for injuries to or death of any person or persons, or loss of or damage to property, to the extent attributable to the negligent acts of Bidder , its subcontractors or its respective agents, servants, or employees or such parties’ related to said Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Bidder is not required to indemnify or hold harmless the County for any liability, damages, claims, suits, liens, and judgments arising from the use, misuse, or disclosure of Confidential Information. The County assumes all risks associated with the disclosure of such information.
perturbation.explanation: This removes the indemnification clause, meaning the Bidder is no longer responsible for damages resulting from their handling of confidential information. This creates a contradiction with the underlying principle of the agreement, especially if the County suffers damages due to the Bidder's actions. It particularly contradicts the 'Purpose' stated in section 1, now that the county has limited ability to ensure the safety and security of Ashburn Sheriff Station.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The purpose of this agreement is to allow the Bidder access to Confidential Information as defined herein detailing the design of the Project for the sole purpose of evaluating the feasibility of potential partnerships related to the Project. This agreement is not intended to ensure the safety and security of the Ashburn Sheriff Station. By signing this agreement, the Bidder is stating that it will handle Confidential Information with reasonable care. Further, the Bidder warrants that its employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients (collectively hereinafter the “Bidder”) will be bound only by the minimal requirements of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Bidder to indemnify the County for damages resulting from the handling of confidential information. The changed text explicitly states that the Bidder is not required to indemnify the County for liabilities arising from the use or disclosure of confidential information, which means the county assumes the risks of handling confidential information. This contradicts the original expectation in the contradicted text that the Bidder will handle confidential information with reasonable care, directly conflicting with the purpose of allowing bidder access to confidential information while limiting the county's ability to ensure the safety and security of the Ashburn Sheriff Station. Therefore, a clear and impactful contradiction exists, as the burden of risk is shifted from the Bidder to the County, contradicting the implicit expectation of care by the Bidder when handling Confidential Information as per the stated Purpose.

perturbation.original_text: A. “Confiden tial Information” as outlined herein shall be used in strict confidence solely for the purpose allotted under this agreement and shall not be disclosed by the Bidder , its employee s, partners, subcontractors, agents, or clients to a third party without the prior written consent of the County with the exception of the following;  It was already in the possession of the receiving party;  It is learned from or become s publicly disclosed by a source with legal right to use and disclose same  It is identical to info rmation which was developed by the receiving party independently of any Confidential Information received from the other party. B. Confidential Information means all information relating to the design, layout and operation of said Project .
perturbation.changed_text: A. "Confidential Information" as outlined herein can be used at the Bidder's discretion. The Bidder, its employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, or clients can disclose it to any third party without needing prior written consent from the County. B. Confidential Information means all information, except information relating to the design, layout, and operation of the project.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows the bidder to use the information at their discretion, eliminating the need to keep it confidential. This directly opposes the 'purpose' of the agreement as stated in section 1, which is to protect the safety and security of the Ashburn Sheriff Station. Also it changes the definition of what constitutes confidential information, creating confusion. If the confidential information isn't the design and layout, what exactly is it?
perturbation.contradicted_text: The purpose of this agreement is to allow the Bidder access to Confidential Information as defined herein detailing the design of the Project for the sole purpose of evaluating the feasibility of potential partnerships related to the Project. This agreement is not intended to ensure the safety and security of the Ashburn Sheriff Station. By signing this agreement, the Bidder is stating that it will handle Confidential Information with reasonable care. Further, the Bidder warrants that its employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients (collectively hereinafter the “Bidder”) will be bound only by the minimal requirements of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text define Confidential Information and restrict its use to evaluating partnerships related to the Project, further stating the Bidder will handle it with reasonable care and only be bound by minimal confidentiality. The changed text, however, gives the bidder complete discretion over Confidential Information, allowing disclosure to any third party without consent and changes the definition of what is confidential. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the obligations related to confidentiality are directly opposed between the changed and contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The governing terms and conditions of this agreement are expressly limited to the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement. This agreement constitutes the complete integration of all oral and written documents, is the entire and final agreement between the parties and may be amended only in writing signed by authorized officials of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The governing terms and conditions of this agreement are not limited to the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement. This agreement does not constitute the complete integration of all oral and written documents, is not the entire and final agreement between the parties and may be amended orally by any representative of either party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the entire agreement section, it opens the door for oral agreements and side deals, which completely negates the purpose of having a written contract and introduces uncertainty regarding the binding nature of the other terms. This contradicts the purpose in section 1, by giving the freedom to oral agreements which can result in confidential information disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The purpose of this agreement is to allow the Bidder access to Confidential Information as defined herein detailing the design of the Project for the sole purpose of evaluating the feasibility of potential partnerships related to the Project. This agreement is not intended to ensure the safety and security of the Ashburn Sheriff Station. By signing this agreement, the Bidder is stating that it will handle Confidential Information with reasonable care. Further, the Bidder warrants that its employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients (collectively hereinafter the “Bidder”) will be bound only by the minimal requirements of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement does *not* constitute the complete and final agreement between the parties and *can* be amended orally by any representative. The original text states the opposite. The contradicted text deals with confidential information being released. By allowing oral agreements and negating the purpose of the entire agreement, this directly contradicts the Bidder's ability to 'handle Confidential Information with reasonable care' as stated in the contradicted text. This also creates uncertainty on who is bound by the minimal requirements of confidentiality.
